entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI 🤖🧠
entry.content: finally able to escape room after multiple try. thank you for super challenges. It keeps me engage and motived to fix issues. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Creating Status Profile
entry.content: It's missing function. Suggest you create feature request.

entry.title: Re: Business Application Studio - modules not recognized...
entry.content: The issue has been resolved for me. Perhaps a fix update was performed. I hope it works for you too.

entry.title: Re: Production Order consumption from wrong place
entry.content:
When you do the CO11N or Goods issue, the default storage location is in the production order, which defined by the Production version and MRP2 View, and your expectation is that when you do the goods issue, the storage location must be "Storage location" in production order.

You can use a BADI "MB_MIGO_BADI"  with T-code "SE18" to do an enhancement to check the storages location in Goods issue must be the "storage location" in production order, thank you!

 

Operation Steps

  1. Create a Class: Use the SE24 transaction code in the SAP system to create the  class ex.  "zcl_261_stock_location_check".
  2. Implement the BADI: Use the SE18 transaction code to find MB_MIGO_BADI, create a new implementation, and specify this class as the implementation class.
  3. Activate: Activate the class and the BADI implementation.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting are the entered comments in GRDC avai...
entry.content: Sorry the comment on GRDC is only possible in Form. You cannot import them to others system like SAC.

entry.title: Re: Restore deleted chart of accounts
entry.content: @raymond_giuseppi NO the exact steps that i performed are i enter to OBD4 then new entries, create 5 chart of account groups then saved it. but when i see the the groups that i created were wrong i enter to OBD4 than FILA(the company that i created) then select all then delete the groups.

Would like to raise a product support regarding CHARM and JIRA Integration

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CHARM and JIRA Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

In standard, there are no documents for the integration between ChaRM and JIRA, it is a consulting question. I would recommend please involve consulting service team for specific help.

BR.

entry.title: Re: STMS Reconfiguration for SAP System after Refresh.
entry.content: Great Blog, Thank you very much..!

Matthias

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Public Cloud, Group Reporting: What is tar...
entry.content: To set up versions J10, J1R, J1S, J20, and J2R, refer to the instructions in the 'Additional Settings for Multiple Group Currencies and Restatement (Optional)' section of 1SG test script and create them manually.

entry.title: Re: Contact person role BUP001 not populated using Custom...
entry.content:
Hi
Unfortunately, the role BUP001 is not added automatically when migrating the contact persons.
We have struggled with the same issue: Solved: Migration of contact persons - SAP Community
You will have to find a way to enrich the contact persons with the BUP001 role as a post-migration step. 
Consider looking into some of the Mass apps or transactions.  

Best Regards,
Gitte

entry.title: Re: Delete Homepage Career Site Builder
entry.content:
Thank you for verifying that you can't delete a site. That's a shame.

I don't think posting the same content as on the global site is good, as it suggests to the google algorithm that there are duplicate websites which is bad for the seo ranking. 
Hopefully SAP will consider the option to delete homepages, as it would be very helpful!

entry.title: Re: Mapping of SAML attributes with XSUAA JWT in Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Hi Prashant,

Amazing blog! It was of great help! I was able to retrieve these attributes from the service in DEV subaccount consuming them from Postman and Workzone but is strange that deploying the service in TEST subaccount, I'm able to retrieve these attributes calling the services from Postman but not calling the services from Workzone. Any idea with some missing configuration? Maybe in Workzone?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: System not responding when export from SAP to Excel
entry.content: Hi, just wondering if your problem got resolved ? My SAP had same problem now...

entry.title: Re: Factory Action on multiple instances does not refresh...
entry.content: @ClearQueries thanks for the video, but unfortunately it does not help. I checked and - besides you using UUIDs as a key in the view - we are doing exactly the same, as you can see from the code i posed in the initial post. I also confirmed in debugger, that the correct cid and key is set. Do you have a complete working sample of local annotations in any repository i could check out? To see if mine somehow differs from a working solution?

entry.title: Billing Block on SAP ISU
entry.content:
In SAP IS-U (Industry Solution for Utilities), a Billing Block is a technical setting used to stop the processing of billing documents for certain customers or contract accounts. It serves as a control tool to prevent bill generation until the issue causing the block is resolved. Billing blocks are commonly applied for reasons such as inaccurate meter readings, unusually high consumption, outstanding payments, or data inconsistencies.

 

Before we proceed, let’s clarify some essential SAP ISU terms:

  • Contract Account (CA): The customer’s account for utility services.
  • Contract: The agreement for utility services between the provider and the customer.

Now, let’s move on to applying a billing block.

  • Find the Contract via Contract Account
  1. Open Transaction Code CAA3.
  2. Enter the Contract Account (CA) and press Enter.
  3. Click on Environment at the top menu, and you will see the associated Contract.
  • Apply the Billing Block
  1. Open Transaction Code ES21.
  2. Enter the Contract you found in Step 1 and press Enter.
  3. Navigate to Page 2.
  4. Enter the Bill Block Reason in the designated field.
  5. Click Save to confirm the changes.

A success message will appear at the bottom of the screen.

Thanks for reading.

entry.title: API Error"Error in document: BKPFF $ 0M50RVE"
entry.content:
HI 各位老師，我目前想透過 JournalEntryBulkCreateReques來寫入KR的供應商發票，但遇到了錯誤訊息"Error in document: BKPFF $ 0M50RVE"，以下是我的Request內容，再請各位老師協助。

<soapenv:Envelope
 xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
 xmlns:sfin="http://sap.com/xi/SAPSCORE/SFIN">
    <soapenv:Header/>
    <soapenv:Body>
        <sfin:JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>
            <MessageHeader>
                <CreationDateTime>2024-07-24T11:00:00.1234567Z</CreationDateTime>
            </MessageHeader>
            <JournalEntryCreateRequest>
                <MessageHeader>
                    <CreationDateTime>2024-07-24T11:00:00.1234567Z</CreationDateTime>
                </MessageHeader>
                <JournalEntry>
                    <OriginalReferenceDocumentType>BKPFF</OriginalReferenceDocumentType>
                    <BusinessTransactionType>RFBU</BusinessTransactionType>
                    <AccountingDocumentType>KR</AccountingDocumentType>
                    <DocumentHeaderText>供應商發票測試</DocumentHeaderText>
                    <CreatedByUser>Martin</CreatedByUser>
                    <CompanyCode>6310</CompanyCode>
                    <DocumentDate>2024-05-15</DocumentDate>
                    <PostingDate>2024-07-15</PostingDate>
                    <TaxDeterminationDate>2024-07-15</TaxDeterminationDate>
                    <DocumentReferenceID>ZW35429603</DocumentReferenceID>
                    <CreditorItem>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <Creditor>TW2503211</Creditor>
                        <AltvRecnclnAccts>21100000</AltvRecnclnAccts>
                        <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="TWD">-24000</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
                        <DebitCreditCode>H</DebitCreditCode>
                    </CreditorItem>
                    <Item>
                        <GLAccount>12600000</GLAccount>
                        <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="TWD">4000</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
                        <Tax>
                            <TaxCode>VZ</TaxCode>
                        </Tax>
                        <AccountAssignment>
                            <CostCenter>63101101</CostCenter>
                        </AccountAssignment>
                    </Item>
                    <Item>
                        <GLAccount>61401000</GLAccount>
                        <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="TWD">20000</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
                        <Tax>
                            <TaxCode>VZ</TaxCode>
                        </Tax>
                        <AccountAssignment>
                            <CostCenter>63101101</CostCenter>
                        </AccountAssignment>
                        <DocumentItemText>123</DocumentItemText>
                    </Item>
                    <!--<ProductTaxItem>
      <TaxCode>V6</TaxCode>
      <TaxItemClassification>VST</TaxItemClassification>
      <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="TWD">1200</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
      <DebitCreditCode>S</DebitCreditCode>
      <TaxBaseAmountInTransCrcy currencyCode="TWD">24000</TaxBaseAmountInTransCrcy>
                        <ProductiveTaxText>test</ProductiveTaxText>
     </ProductTaxItem>:-->
                </JournalEntry>
            </JournalEntryCreateRequest>
        </sfin:JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>
    </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

Thank you and Kind regards,
Deyana

entry.title: Re: Embedded Intelligent Scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @RobynCouch ,

As far as I know, these machine learning scenarios have been introduced years ago, they are not license required. They are not LLM based which might be another story.

Regards,

Qi

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with Edge Integration Cell on AWS: A Setup Guide Using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: Amazon offers EKS Anywhere (Kubernetes On-Premises - Amazon EKS Anywhere - AWS) to truly deploy Kubernetes on-premise (in one's company's own data centre). Can BTP Edge Integration Cell (EIC) run on EKS Anywhere? Are there particular gotcha's for such deployment?

entry.title: Re: Understanding SAP’s Product Strategy for Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) Solutions
entry.content: Indeed, SAPs commitment to GRC (Access Control, Risk- and Audit management, Business Integrity Screening and UI-Masking and -logging) for SAP S/4HANA extends to the lifecycle of SAP S/4HANA. All customers can count on future releases. Tax Compliance is an exception, as this module is captured by the SAP product SAP Risk- and Compliance Management (RAM), our SaaS-product based on BTP. All customers using Tax Compliance On Premises are encouraged to make use of the easy to deploy and fast time to value application RAM going forward. Additionally, SAP RAM gives all customers also the option to move their management of controls into the cloud. This gives companies a choice for a fully operated as-a-service software for GRC in addition to GRC for Private Cloud as described in this article.

entry.title: SSCUI: Maintain Route Determination维护路线确定
entry.content:
功能介绍

S/4HANA Cloud供应链中路线确定是一个重要流程, 用于自动为外向交货选择合适的运输路线。如果路线确定执行不正确, 会对业务产生以下影响:

  • 路线决定了关键的运输信息,如运输时间和方式。错误的路线可能导致交货日期不准确和不适当的运输方法。
  • 需要人工干预: 如果系统持续确定错误的路线, 可能需要频繁的手动更正,增加工作量和出错可能性。
  • 成本计算不准确: 由于路线影响运输成本, 错误的确定可能导致成本计算和定价不准确。

验证路线确定因素的配置：

  • 装运点数据(国家和出发区)
  • 销售凭证和客户主数据中的装运条件
  • 物料主数据中的运输组
  • 收货方数据(国家和运输区)
  • 路线是否在路线主数据中正确维护

当客户维护了错误路线确定记录时，客户需要删除或者更改这条记录，避免对客户带来糟糕的用户体验。

2502版本更新后，客户可以在SSCUI:Maintain Route Determination维护路线确定中删除不合适的记录。

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: Contact person role BUP001 not populated using Custom...

entry.title: Re: certain report cannot detect ApplicationResources.pro...
entry.content:
Hi Karl,

Thank you for your detailed instructions—it worked like a charm!

Additionally, I discovered another solution by modifying the .rptdesign XML as follows:

<simple-property-list name="includeResource">
<value>ApplicationResources</value>
</simple-property-list>

Thank you again!

Best, Jessica

entry.title: What is the purpose of recognition and re-recognition validation?
Re: How to convert .PPTX to PDF
entry.content: Search for BC-XDC interface. That's SAP way to send attachments to external converter for printing.

entry.title: Mastering Test Data Container Usage in SAP Post-Upgrade Testing: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
SAP Post-Upgrade Tests (PUT) is a crucial step in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud upgrade process, enabling system stability and business continuity. One of the most important factors influencing the efficiency of this phase is the effective use of Test Data Container (TDC). When leveraged correctly, TDCs can streamline automation efforts, reduce manual intervention, and enhance test accuracy and efficacy.

As an Expert in Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, I’m excited to share practical insights and best practices on optimizing TDC usage in PUT.

The Foundation: Identifying PUT Processes

The journey to efficient TDC usage begins with a comprehensive inventory of all PUT processes selected for execution. This initial step is crucial as it lays the groundwork for effective test data management within your TDC variant. By having a clear picture of all processes, you can ensure that no test scenario is overlooked and that your test data strategy is all-encompassing.

The Devil in the Details: Reviewing and Maintaining TDC Bindings

Once you have your process list, it's time to dive deep into each test process. The goal here is to identify and maintain all TDC bindings meticulously. This involves ensuring that all required and relevant field values are accurately maintained in the standard variant 'Variant_XX' of the respective standard TDCs. This step is critical in minimising data inconsistencies that could potentially derail your test execution.

Snapshot 1 showing relevant filed values being maintained in Variant_XX

The Safety Net: System Check on PUT Trigger

As you trigger PUT, the system performs an automatic validation - a crucial safety net in your testing process. This check ensures that all necessary TDC binding data is available in the standard TDC variant. If all bound fields have maintained data, the system proceeds with test execution using this data, bypassing the data picking logic. However, if any field is missing, the system falls back to the intelligent data picking logic, ensuring uninterrupted test execution.

The Time-Saver: Auto-Population of TDC Data

To further streamline the process, SAP offers an option for auto-population of bound test data containers. By providing consent, you allow the system to automatically* fill the standard TDC variant 'Variant_XX' with test data from the data picking logic  for standard post-upgrade test automates, if data isn't already maintained. This feature not only reduces manual effort but also ensures a more structured and automated test data management process.

Snapshot 2 showcasing provision of consent for auto-population of test data in Post-Upgrade Tests app

*Please review the automatically filled data in the variant for your automates

 The Maintenance Routine: Regular Data Review

As with any robust testing strategy, regular maintenance is key. For each release, it's crucial to review the data maintained in the standard variant and update it as necessary before triggering PUT. This proactive approach minimizes failures due to data inconsistencies and keeps your testing process smooth and efficient.

Conclusion:

Implementing these best practices for TDC usage in SAP Post-Upgrade Testing (PUT) can significantly enhance your testing efficiency. By leveraging system validations, automated data population, and structured test processes, you can achieve seamless execution while reducing manual effort and maintaining high data accuracy.

Remember, the key to successful PUT lies not just in automation, but in smart automation. By following these guidelines, you're not just running tests; you're optimizing your entire testing strategy, saving time, reducing errors, and ultimately, delivering a more robust SAP system to your organization.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, so too must our testing strategies. Stay tuned for more insights on SAP test automation, and don't hesitate to share your experiences with TDC usage in the comments below. Together, we can continue to refine and improve our SAP testing practices.

Useful Links :

  • Test Data Container Help Document
  • Post-Upgrade Tests Help Document

entry.title: Introducing SAP Application Extension Methodology version 2.3
entry.content:
What’s New for SAP Application Extension Methodology

With the SAP Application Extension Methodology version 2.3, we have made some changes to the technology-agnostic extension tasks and refined the Extension Technology Mapping. For example, we introduced new extension tasks around the topic of Event-Driven Architecture.

If you are still using an older version of the Technology Mapping, I highly recommend checking out our new version and to learn about the latest innovations and technologies on SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform.

 

Extension Architecture Guide

Our Methodology is part of the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. With this Framework, we provide a central access point for architects, developers, and administrators to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP.

We now offer a dedicated topic on “Getting Started with Extensibility”. This guide provides a simplified introduction to extending SAP’s ERP offerings. It outlines the SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility model with SAP Business Technology Platform, which applies to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (GROW with SAP), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (RISE with SAP), and SAP S/4HANA.

Getting Started with Extensibility

With SAP Build, you can create extensions that run on-stack in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA, and/or side-by-side on SAP BTP.

Depending on your use cases and the capabilities required, you can choose a flexible combination of on-stack and side-by-side extensions.

 

Next Steps:

Learn more about the SAP Application Extension Methodology on the SAP Help Portal.
Learn more about the Extension Architecture Guide on the SAP Help Portal.

Feel free to like, comment and ask your questions!

Talk to you soon,
Marco

Product Manager SAP Business Technology Platform
Join me online: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marcomatha/

entry.title: Re: SoD without GRC Tool
entry.content:
Hi Caiogvn,

indeed that is difficult, that's why Access Governance tools for SAP have been developed 🙂

Nevertheless, you should be able to create a rough manual-intensive and error-prone tool extracting data about users and roles from an SAP system and using e.g. MS Excel.
But it requires a lot of work anyway.

Hope it helps and that your company can decide to adopt a GRC tool soon!

entry.title: Re: New Trusted Authentication for Single Server installation and Entra Id
entry.content: Hi John,

entry.title: Re: Widgets in SAP Business One HANA retrieves informatio...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

all the widgets and KPIs are based on queries. From your description it would seem that these queries have a hardcoded schema in them, so I would guess these are user-created KPIs and widgets. It is best practice to avoid using the chema in Queries when writing them in the client, as it would automatically take the data from current database.

Example of a hardcoded schema:

SELECT * FROM "SCHEMA"."ORDR"

To get the data from the current database you can simply use:

SELECT * FROM "ORDR"

So the solution in your case would be to remove the database name from queries these widgets are based on.

 

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: New Trusted Authentication for Single Server installation and Entra Id
entry.content: Hi John, thank you very much for your message. What I find a bit frustrating that SAP has provided a SAP Security Patch Day in February, stating that is important to update BO... https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/knowledge-base/security-notes-news/february-2025.html

entry.title: DMS Content Server migration to BTP DMS Repository
entry.content:
General Introduction

ABCD company - DMS Content Server migration to BTP DMS Repository - 

Steps

  1. Upgrade existing non-production Content Server 6.5 to Content Server 7.5x
  2. Connect non-production upgraded Content Server 7.5x to ECC6 non-production System(s).
    1. Please verify with Basis team that you can connect upgraded Content Server 7.5x to ECC6 system.
  3. BASIS team shall test and validate upgraded server in ECC6 non-production System(s).
  4. Connect non-production upgraded Content Server 7.5x to S/4 non-production System(s).
  5. Migrate limited number of attachments from upgraded Content Server connected to S/4 non-production System to BTP DMS Repository option.
  6. Upgrade existing production Content Server 6.5 to Content Server 7.5x
  7. Connect production Content Server 7.5x to existing ECC6 Production System.
    1. Please verify with Basis team that you can connect upgraded Content Server 7.5x to ECC6 system.
  8. During S/4 go-live cutover activities, we should change connection of upgraded production Content Server 7.5x from ECC6 Production to S/4 Production System.
  9. After go-live, migrate attachments from upgraded Content Server connected to S/4 production System to BTP DMS Repository option.

Please find the Architecture diagram below 

STEP 1:
create a new BTP subaccount or use the existing BTP subaccount 

SAP BTP Cockpit 

1. Sub-account Overview > Enable Cloud Foundry

2. Create an SPACE > add users to SPACE
3. Entity Assignments and select Entities

Global Account > Entitlements > Entity  Assignments

Click on Configure Entitlements

Click on Add service Plans

Sub-Account > Entitlements > Add Service Plans

Go to SUB account and add into the account

you can see the Services in Entity Assignments

in service marketplace you can see the Application

Instance and Subscription > Create New instance or subscription

click on new instance or subscription & click Next

Instance creating is in progress it took 3 mins to complete 

create Key for instance

 Before that u need to create an Role

Create Role Collection and assign roles

Click on ROLE, select on required Roles need to be added to Role collections

add to Role collection

After save click on back on the DMS_PRD and select another role and ADD

Click on Instances and Subscriptions

Service key need to create

after that login to POST man or Accelerator to create an API 

i have used the Accelerator 

Login to Accelerator: please find the link below for the Accelerator 

Try Out | Administrative Operations | SAP Business Accelerator Hub 

RUN the API successful

 

STEP 2 

login to S/4 HANA system 

Login to BACKEND system S/4 and execute the T-code

OA2C_CONFIG

Activate the services Json

RFC is successful now create the OACO

Create Content Repository in OACO

OR

you can use Onboard Repository using POST man 

 You may use https://www.base64encode.org/  in case encoding is not working in POST / POSTMAN

Note--------------------------------------

Ensure that user executing 'list repositories' or 'onboard repositories' in 'SAP Business Accelerator Hub' shall have required roles assigned in respective sub-account

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

technical configuration steps for BTP DMS, based on my experience. I recently had the opportunity to configure DMS for a client, where I faced several challenges. Through this process, I documented my learnings, and the steps involved.

Please feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need further clarification.

Best regards,
Allam Prabhu

entry.title: Re: Sales rebate processing - condition contract pricing
entry.content:
Hi Patryk,

I created an incident since this is very urgent. Unfortunately, a custom condition is not possible in condition contracts in S4HANA Public cloud. 

 "A custom condition type can be created in cloud. However, to use it in a condition contract it needs to be
assigned to a condition type group and a condition table. This SSCUI screen is not released for modification 
in cloud version. Therefore, the custom condition type cannot be used in condition contracts."

BR 
Katja

entry.title: Re: External Numbering in RAP.
entry.content:
Hi Gireesh,

The issue that you are facing might be because a draft instance already exists with the same external key that you are trying to create.

Real11 Referr𝑎l Code  

IHD100

Refer and Earn

Rs.200

entry.title: Deploy SAP Fiori App on S/4 HANA Public Cloud with SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a weird problem when trying to deploy SAP Fiori app through SAP business application studio on S/4 HANA public cloud development tenant (080).  I am following the below tutorial but getting the error on Step 5 during the deployment.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-s4hanacloud-procurement-purchasereq-shop-ui.html

I have developed RAP application in Eclipse IDE and have connected SAP BTP and S/4 HANA cloud application to deploy the application. App has built successfully but when trying to deploy same, getting the below error.

error builder:custom deploy-to-abap Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'bind').
Please help me to understand the error and the steps to resolve the same.

Thank you in advance!

Thanks.

entry.title: Could you please tell me how to register an order slip and how to process it afterwards when direct
entry.content:
Could you please tell me how to register an order slip and how to process it afterwards when direct shipping to the supplier is done at the time of ordering, rather than starting from an order receipt?
I understand that BDK and BD3 Scope Items are applicable when ordering.
I would be grateful if you could provide me with Scope Items and materials that describe direct shipping to the supplier when ordering.

entry.title: SAP IBP Production Source Item Sub standard integration
entry.content:
Good day

Is there any standart dataflow (for example using addon) for integration alternative materials from S/4 to SAP IBP - CPI DS?
Masterdata Type - Production Source Item Sub

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: FIORI APP Preise verwalten - Verkauf F4111
entry.content: Hello Ariane4everSuccess,

entry.title: Re: In SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud, What is A10 and how do I...
entry.content: The group currency in SAP pre-delivered cons. versions is determined by cloud customers when specifying the group currency in "Primary Financial Settings". Group currency varies customer by customer. 1SG test script provides data sets and expected consolidation result with group currency EURO.  To provide generic test procedures fit for all cloud customers, testers are asked to create a consolidation version A10 with group currency EURO manually following the instruction of 1SG test script. As the note said, this is for testing purpose with SAP provided data sets.

entry.title: Re: FIORI APP Preise verwalten - Verkauf F4111
entry.content:
Hello Ariane4everSuccess,

can you please describe the error in more detail. Ideally with a screenshot of the error message.

Best regards

Patrick

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Public Cloud, Group Reporting: What is tar...
entry.content:
Hello 

the version J10 is used to show and explain business scenario based on extension version(s). J10 is the standard version for extension versions as described in test script. 

the version J10 should be available as of release 2408. If scope item 1SG is activated in a prior release, you have to copy J10 from Y10 and adjust as described in test script. 

If version J10 is not available and you cannot copy from Y10 to J10, raise an incident component FIN-CS-CO-2CL.

entry.title: Re: IBP - Standard method to Deleting key Figures and the...
entry.content:
Hi 

Since you also want to delete the Planning Objects, I suggest you use the Purge Key Figure, which has an option to do so.

Moreover, since Snap Shot KF is used only on 1 Planning Level, Purge KF seems to be the best option.

 

V

entry.title: Re: In SAP Public Cloud, how to update the currency for cost and budget, which currency should be us
entry.content: Hello, Thank you very much for your question. Unfortunately, the question did not reach the right experts because of the missing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance tag. It has now been forwarded to the experts. Sorry for the delay! Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Goldenes Zertifikat für affinis: Meilenstein einer 20-jährigen Erfolgsgeschichte
entry.content:
Seit fast 20 Jahren verbindet uns eine erfolgreiche Partnerschaft mit affinis enterprise services GmbH, die auf hoher fachlicher Kompetenz, Vertrauen und einer engen Zusammenarbeit basiert. Mit der 10. Re-Zertifizierung hat affinis nun das goldene Zertifikat erhalten – ein Meilenstein, der die langjährige Qualität und Kontinuität dieser Partnerschaft unterstreicht. Anlässlich der Zertifikatsübergabe hatten wir die Möglichkeit, mit affinis über ihre Erfahrungen, Entwicklungen und zukünftigen Perspektiven zu sprechen.

SAP: Zunächst einmal vielen Dank, dass Sie sich die Zeit für dieses Interview nehmen. Die affinis Unternehmensgruppe ist seit fast 20 Jahren SAP-zertifizierter Operations Service Partner und die affinis enterprise services GmbH hat kürzlich das unternehmensgruppenweite goldene Zertifikat nach der 10. Re-Zertifizierung erhalten. Es ist beeindruckend, diese lange Partnerschaft zu feiern. Vielleicht möchten Sie damit beginnen, Ihr Unternehmen vorzustellen und uns einen Überblick über die von Ihnen angebotenen Services zu geben.

affinis: Sehr gerne! Die affinis enterprise services GmbH als Teil der affinis Unternehmensgruppe ist ein führender Anbieter von IT-Services, der sich auf die Bereitstellung von Hosting- und Outsourcing-Dienstleistungen für SAP-Systeme spezialisiert hat. Unser Hauptfokus liegt darauf, Unternehmen bei der effizienten Nutzung ihrer SAP-Infrastrukturen zu unterstützen. Wir bieten maßgeschneiderte Lösungen, die auf die individuellen Bedürfnisse unserer Kunden abgestimmt sind, sei es im Bereich SAP S/4HANA, Cloud-Dienste oder Managed Services. Unsere langjährige Erfahrung und Expertise in der SAP-Technologie ermöglichen es uns, erstklassige Services zu liefern, die Unternehmen dabei helfen, ihre IT-Kosten zu optimieren und die Effizienz zu steigern.

SAP: Im Jahr 2006 haben Sie sich erstmals für die Zertifizierung Ihres Hosting Services entschieden. Was war damals Ihre Motivation, diesen Schritt zu gehen, und wie bewerten Sie diese Entscheidung heute?

affinis: Die Entscheidung, unseren Hosting Service 2006 zertifizieren zu lassen, war eine strategische Maßnahme, um unseren Kunden höchste Qualität und Sicherheit bieten zu können. SAP-Zertifizierungen sind ein klarer Indikator für Vertrauen und Qualität, was sowohl für uns als auch für unsere Kunden von enormer Bedeutung ist. Rückblickend war es definitiv die richtige Entscheidung, da uns die Zertifizierung nicht nur geholfen hat, unser Serviceangebot kontinuierlich zu verbessern, sondern auch das Vertrauen unserer Kunden gestärkt hat. Heute sind wir stolz darauf, diese Zertifizierung immer wieder zu erhalten und sogar mit unserem goldenen Status nach der 10. Re-Zertifizierung ausgezeichnet zu werden.

Zertifikatsübergabe in Walldorf, März 2025

 

SAP: Gerade haben Sie die SAP HANA Operations-Zertifizierung erhalten. Was war ausschlaggebend für diesen Schritt, und was bedeutet diese Zertifizierung für Ihr Unternehmen?

affinis: Die Entscheidung, uns für SAP HANA Operations zertifizieren zu lassen, war eine logische Weiterentwicklung unserer Strategie. SAP HANA hat sich als eine der leistungsfähigsten Datenbank-technologien etabliert, und unsere Kunden benötigen zunehmend maßgeschneiderte Lösungen, um die Vorteile dieser Technologie vollständig auszuschöpfen. Diese Zertifizierung ermöglicht es uns, unseren Kunden noch effizientere und leistungsstärkere Lösungen und Services anzubieten. Zudem positioniert es uns als einen der führenden Anbieter im Bereich SAP HANAOperations, was uns einen klaren Wettbewerbs-vorteil verschafft.

SAP: Wie beurteilen Sie die Audits, die Sie im Rahmen der Zertifizierungen durchlaufen haben? Konnte Ihr Unternehmen aus den Audits und dem erhaltenen Feedback neue Erkenntnisse für Ihr Service-Angebot gewinnen?

affinis: Die Audits sind immer eine wertvolle Gelegenheit zur Reflexion und Verbesserung. SAP hat sehr hohe Standards, und die Audits fordern uns immer wieder heraus, unsere Prozesse und Servicequalität weiter zu optimieren. Das Feedback, das wir erhalten, hilft uns, Schwachstellen zu identifizieren und kontinuierliche Verbesserungen vorzunehmen. Es ist für uns ein wesentlicher Bestandteil unserer Strategie, aus den Audits zu lernen und unser Service-Angebot so anzupassen, dass wir den ständig wachsenden Anforderungen der SAP-Welt gerecht werden.

SAP: Spielt die Zertifizierung bei Ihren Marketing-Aktivitäten eine Rolle? Bewerben Sie aktiv Ihren Status als SAP-zertifizierter Outsourcing-Anbieter?

affinis: Ja, die Zertifizierung spielt eine wichtige Rolle in unserer Marketingstrategie. Als SAP-zertifizierter Outsourcing-Anbieter bieten wir unseren Kunden ein hohes Maß an Sicherheit und Vertrauen. Wir bewerben diesen Status aktiv, sowohl auf unserer Website als auch in unserer Kommunikation mit potenziellen Kunden. Die SAP-Zertifizierungen sind ein klarer Nachweis dafür, dass wir über die nötige Expertise und Erfahrung verfügen, um anspruchsvolle IT-Infrastrukturen zu betreuen und Lösungen anzubieten, die den SAP-Standards entsprechen.

SAP: SAP richtet seine Strategie zunehmend auf die Cloud aus und ermutigt Partner, ihre Kunden aktiv bei der Migration zu unterstützen. Planen Sie, Ihre Services stärker auf die Cloud auszurichten, oder bieten Sie bereits Lösungen an, die Kunden in ihrer Transformation begleiten?

affinis: Die Cloud ist ein wesentlicher Bestandteil der zukünftigen IT-Strategien vieler Unternehmen, und wir haben diesen Trend frühzeitig erkannt. Wir bieten bereits heute Lösungen, die unsere Kunden bei der Migration in die Cloud unterstützen. Unsere Services ermöglichen es Unternehmen, SAP-Anwendungen sicher und skalierbar in der Cloud zu betreiben. Darüber hinaus arbeiten wir kontinuierlich an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Cloud-Angebote, um unseren Kunden bei der Transformation noch bessere Lösungen zu bieten. Unsere Partnerschaft mit SAP und die zertifizierten SAP-Cloud-Dienste spielen dabei eine zentrale Rolle, um unsere Kunden in ihrer Cloud-Transformation effektiv zu begleiten.

SAP: Vielen Dank für Ihre wertvollen Einblicke. Abschließend, was sind die nächsten Schritte für die affinis enterprise services GmbH in Bezug auf die Zusammenarbeit mit SAP?

affinis: Wir werden weiterhin eng mit SAP zusammenarbeiten und unsere Expertise insbesondere im Bereich der Cloud- und SAP-HANA-Dienste – vornehmlich RISE und GROW – weiter ausbauen. Unser Ziel ist es, unseren Kunden weiterhin innovative, effiziente und sichere Lösungen anzubieten, die ihre SAP-Systeme auf die nächste Stufe heben. Die fortlaufende Verbesserung unseres Service-Portfolios und die Anpassung an die sich ständig weiterentwickelnde SAP-Technologie sind wesentliche Bestandteile unserer Strategie. Wir freuen uns auf die nächsten Jahre der Zusammenarbeit mit SAP und darauf, unsere Partnerschaft weiter zu  vertiefen.

 

Informationen zum Zertifizierungsprogramm für Operations Partner und eine Übersicht über alle zertifizierten Partner finden Sie auf unserem SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Erfahren Sie mehr auf PartnerEdge.com

entry.title: Re: 在S4 hana cloud 公有云版本中，创建生产（CO01）订单能否将”收货“部分的”批次“字段通过配置设置为必填项？如何实现以及如何配置？谢谢！
entry.content: English translation: In the S4 hana cloud public cloud version, is it possible to create a production (CO01) order to make the ‘Batch’ field ‘partial’ of Goods Receipt mandatory via configuration? How is it implemented and how to configure it? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: unable to drill down in a Crystal report chart
entry.content: Hi Don, the problem happens when accessing via a browser on the server itself (Windows Server 2019) and from a couple of different laptops (Windows 11), thanks, Keith

entry.title: Re: Downpayment for Poland
entry.content:
Hello,

The pricing procedure RVAB02, which you are referring to, is explicitly assigned to document type FAF - downpayment invoice. This pricing procedure is necessary to calculate the net and tax amounts and post the downpayment as deferred revenue. However, you can still use your customer-specific pricing procedure in sales orders and other billing document types, as the new pricing procedure is used only in billing type FAF. 

Regards

entry.title: SAP DRC with SAP CCM Settlements
entry.content:
Hi, we are currently in the ERP to S4HANA transition and already using SAP DRC Document and Reporting Compliance in different countries.
With S4 we will be using SAP CCM Condition Contract Management as a replacement for the old VBO3 rebate agreements. Now we have found out that with CCM the settlements are posting directly to FI/Finance. Therefore we need to have the possibility to have the DRC supported for the settlements for all countries.

According to our analysis, several but not all countries are supported to post settlement documents with DRC - customizable with table EDOTMFKV.

What is your experience with this issue. Is it safe to use CCM settlements for all DRC countries or should we look for another settlement procedure?

Any feedback is welcome.
BR, Günter

entry.title: Re: OPEX Cost centre allocation - Advanced formula clarif...
entry.content:
Hi @praveen_kumar334,

let me explain what the code does based on our sample data:
First you need to understand that you need to specify the cost centers which you want to allocate in the data action trigger.
In our case this is cost center 17101750

The allocation weight is maintained in the SAP_FI_IFP_IM_OPEX_PlanAdmin story:

 

And this is the master data maintained in dimension SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT

 

Line 28
First we're aggregating total debit amount on account # - Unassigned / Credit / Variable Measure #M1

Line 30
Then we copy the data from variable measure #M1 to variable measure #M2 and the allocation account maintained for account # - unassigned
as there is no allocation account maintained in our sample data, data on variable measure #M2 = null

Line 31 + Line 33 check if an allocation account for account # - unassigned is maintained, which is not the case in our sample data
@DEFAULT_ALLOC_ACCOUNT_EXISTS = null *0 +1 = null +0 = 0

this part of the code is important to not loose any data and ensure that the data is allocated according the master data.

The following lines are aggregating the debit amounts over activity, partner objects, profit center and functional area, but keeps cost center and account

This is our most complex data action and I hope the logic is now clear.
We're not aggregating over cost centers or time, but, in our example we're grouping two source accounts (Payroll Expenses - Salaries and Travel Expenses - Miscellaneous) into one allocation account (94202000 Assessment Quality costs).

I highly recommend the below blog post if you're not clear about the resultlookup and aggregation of dimensions 
Advanced Formulas – How they work.

I hope this helps to better understand the logic.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: #CoE#HSQC if app Upload Material Inventory Price can change inventory price for special stock
entry.content: Hello, Thank you very much for your question and sorry for the delay. Your question has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: How to import a BC app based on RAP/SMBC/TOBJ with abapGit
entry.content:
Introduction

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a business configuration maintenance object (SMBC), a transport object (TOBJ) is generated by default and used in the behavior implementation.

While the community version of abapGit supports export/import of TOBJ, this is not the case in the pre-installed cloud version of abapGit.

This blog explains what you can do as a workaround when cloning a Git repository with TOBJ to an ABAP platform system with preinstalled cloud version of abapGit.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP BTP, ABAP environment 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Workaround

The only way to create a TOBJ in BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public is to generate an SMBC object using the ADT wizard.

  1. Create a dummy table that meets the prerequisites of the wizard.
  2. Run the wizard. In the "BC Administration" section of the configuration screen, change the name of the transport object to the name of the TOBJ of the Git repository.
  3. Finish the wizard
  4. Adjust the generated TOBJ to match the one from the Git repository.
  5. Assign the generated TOBJ to the correct package
  6. Delete all other generated repository objects including the dummy table

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales V2 - UI personalization feature
entry.content:
Hello Katarzyna,

 

To do Personalisation in SAP Sales or Service Cloud Version 2, as an administrator, you must create business roles and assign users to the sap.crm.service.personalizationService business services so that they can use Personalization.

https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SALES/ea5ff8b9460a43cb8765a3c07d3421fe/08cd27ac3af34fddb3888cc713bc1315.html?locale=en-US

Best regards

Sara Andersson

entry.title: Re: Customize our solr query
entry.content:
Hello, Follow document provide the overview regarding how to customize your solr search:

(Please check "search API" part for customize query ) https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/aa417173fe4a4ba5a473c93eb730a417/8c54c74f8669101496cf96067a578ef3.html

Best regards,

Kevin

entry.title: Re: Negative value not allowed for depreciation area 93
entry.content: Which amount type gets negative in the transfer posting? What is the error message you get?

entry.title: Re: In SAP S4HANA Public Cloud Group Reporting What do th...
entry.content:
The Jason statements require some coding background. The code-line examples listed in 1SG test script are for sign-based reclass. Configuring such reclassification rules can fill the feature gap that G/L account can't be mapped to different FS items according to the balance of G/L account, on debit side or credit side.

  • For FS Item with attribute value "S-ACCT-DR-BAL":

    • If the aggregated local currency amount (grouped by ConsolidationUnit, ChartOfAccounts, and GLAccount) is negative (< 0), reclassification is triggered.

    • The amount is reclassified to the offset target FS item assigned to the triggering FS item.

  • For FS Item with attribute value "S-ACCT-CR-BAL":

    • If the aggregated local currency amount (grouped by ConsolidationUnit, ChartOfAccounts, and GLAccount) is positive (> 0), reclassification is triggered.

    • The amount is reclassified to the corresponding offset target FS item assigned to the triggering FS item.

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide: Sending SAP Data to SAP Event Mesh in Integration Suite (EMIS). Codeless.
entry.content:
Hi @SRodriguez,

I haven't tried it with Enterprise Event Enablement, but it seems like it should be possible.

The SAP NetWeaver Add-On for Event Enablement enables you to send actual data from your system to EMIS, rather than just a notification and it comes with many more features such as filtering events, switch between real-time messaging and batch processing, monitoring, event reprocessing, custom events, IDocs to JSON, active alerting and more.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Publuc Cloud group reporting - Define Recl...
entry.content:
Help portal explains how Interunit Elimination of Profit in Inventory works. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/985d3be1b1a1497b886017fb1297b4eb.html?version=2502.500

More details can be found in the attached "How to Start Up Interunit Elimination of Profit in Inventory (IPI)_V1.pdf" in note 2659656 - FAQ About SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Group Reporting - SAP for Me

With the assumption that you are using public cloud, please raise a case to SAP with component FIN-CS-COR-RCL-2CL if you have the proper customizing role assigned but you are not able to edit the reclassification method S2015.

SAP Support
Matt

entry.title: Manage forms GRDC (Group reporting)

However this previously worked and i have forms that are live with fiscal year as a dimension

entry.title: Handling Persian Leap Year 1403 in SAP with ZCL_JCAL_UTILITY
entry.content:
In SAP systems, there’s a problem with the Persian calendar for the year 1403: the month of Esfand is set to 29 days when it should be 30 days. This error can mess up date calculations, reports, and scheduling. To fix this, I created the ZCL_JCAL_UTILITY class in SAP ABAP. This class ensures Persian dates are handled correctly, including setting Esfand 1403 to 30 days, and acts as a temporary solution until SAP releases an official patch. Here’s why this class is helpful and how it works.

Why This Class Was Created

In the Persian calendar, Esfand should have 30 days in 1403, but SAP wrongly treats it as 29 days. This causes issues for businesses that depend on accurate dates. The ZCL_JCAL_UTILITY class corrects this by properly converting and managing dates, making sure Esfand is recognized as a 30-day month until SAP provides a permanent fix.

What the Class Does

This class provides methods to manage Persian dates in SAP. Here’s what it includes:

  1. Convert Gregorian to Persian: Turns a Gregorian date into a Persian date (e.g., 20250320 to 1403/12/30).
  2. Convert Persian to Gregorian: Changes a Persian date back to Gregorian for system use.
  3. First Day of Month/Year: Finds the first day of a Persian month or year.
  4. Last Day of Month: Calculates the last day, ensuring Esfand 1403 is set to 30 days.
  5. Days in a Year: Counts days in a Persian year, using the correct month lengths.

These tools make sure SAP handles Persian dates correctly, especially for Esfand 1403, until an official update is available.

How It Helps

  • Fixes Esfand: Corrects Esfand from 29 days to 30 days in 1403.
  • Temporary Solution: Keeps systems working until SAP releases a patch.
  • Easy to Use: Fits smoothly into SAP processes.

The ZCL_JCAL_UTILITY class is a practical fix for businesses using Persian dates in SAP. For more details, see SAP Note 3567476. Below is the code.

CLASS zcl_jcal_utility DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS convert_gregorian_to_shamsi
      IMPORTING REFERENCE(iv_date)        TYPE  datum
      EXPORTING REFERENCE(ev_shamsi_date) TYPE  char10.

    CLASS-METHODS convert_shamsi_to_gregorian
      IMPORTING REFERENCE(iv_shamsi_date) TYPE  char10
      EXPORTING REFERENCE(ev_greg_date)   TYPE  datum.

    CLASS-METHODS first_dayof_month
      IMPORTING  REFERENCE(p_date)       TYPE  d
      EXPORTING  REFERENCE(p_first_days) TYPE  d
      EXCEPTIONS date_conversion_error.

    CLASS-METHODS first_dayof_year
      IMPORTING  REFERENCE(p_date)       TYPE  d
      EXPORTING  REFERENCE(p_first_days) TYPE  d
      EXCEPTIONS date_conversion_error.

    CLASS-METHODS last_dayof_month
      IMPORTING  REFERENCE(p_date)      TYPE  d
      EXPORTING  REFERENCE(p_last_days) TYPE  d
      EXCEPTIONS date_conversion_error.

    CLASS-METHODS get_cal_days_inyrear IMPORTING p_date TYPE d
                                       CHANGING  p_days TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_JCAL_UTILITY IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD convert_gregorian_to_shamsi.
    DATA lt_g_d_m     TYPE TABLE OF i.
    DATA iv_gy        TYPE i.
    DATA iv_gm        TYPE i.
    DATA iv_gd        TYPE i.
    DATA lv_out0      TYPE i.
    DATA lv_out3      TYPE i.
    DATA lv_out1      TYPE i.
    DATA lv_out2      TYPE i.
    DATA lv_year_str  TYPE char4.
    DATA lv_month_str TYPE char2.
    DATA lv_day_str   TYPE char2.

    " Initialize gregorian days per month array
    lt_g_d_m = VALUE #( ( 0 )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( 59 )
                        ( 90 )
                        ( 120 )
                        ( 151 )
                        ( 181 )
                        ( 212 )
                        ( 243 )
                        ( 273 )
                        ( 304 )
                        ( 334 ) ).
    iv_gy = iv_date+0(4).
    iv_gm = iv_date+4(2).
    iv_gd = iv_date+6(2).
    " Calculate initial year
    lv_out0 = COND #( WHEN iv_gm > 2 THEN iv_gy + 1 ELSE iv_gy ).

    " Main calculation
    lv_out3 = 355666 + ( 365 * iv_gy ) +
              ( ( lv_out0 + 3 ) DIV 4 ) -
              ( ( lv_out0 + 99 ) DIV 100 ) +
              ( ( lv_out0 + 399 ) DIV 400 ) +
              iv_gd +
              lt_g_d_m[ iv_gm ].

    lv_out0 = -1595 + ( 33 * ( lv_out3 DIV 12053 ) ).
    lv_out3 = lv_out3 MOD 12053.
    lv_out0 += ( 4 * ( lv_out3 DIV 1461 ) ).
    lv_out3 = lv_out3 MOD 1461.

    IF lv_out3 > 365.
      lv_out0 += ( ( lv_out3 - 1 ) DIV 365 ).
      lv_out3 = ( lv_out3 - 1 ) MOD 365.
    ENDIF.

    IF lv_out3 < 186.
      lv_out1 = 1 + ( lv_out3 DIV 31 ).
      lv_out2 = 1 + ( lv_out3 MOD 31 ).
    ELSE.
      lv_out1 = 7 + ( ( lv_out3 - 186 ) DIV 30 ).
      lv_out2 = 1 + ( ( lv_out3 - 186 ) MOD 30 ).
    ENDIF.

    lv_year_str = lv_out0.
    lv_month_str = lv_out1.
    lv_day_str = lv_out2.
    IF lv_out1 < 10.
      lv_month_str = |0{ lv_month_str }|.
    ENDIF.
    IF lv_out2 < 10.
      lv_day_str = |0{ lv_day_str }|.
    ENDIF.
    ev_shamsi_date = |{ lv_year_str }/{ lv_month_str }/{ lv_day_str }|.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD convert_shamsi_to_gregorian.
    DATA iv_jy    TYPE i.
    DATA iv_jm    TYPE i.
    DATA iv_jd    TYPE i.
    DATA lv_jy    TYPE i.
    DATA lv_out2  TYPE i.
    DATA lv_out0  TYPE i.
    DATA lv_leap  TYPE i.
    DATA lt_sal_a TYPE TABLE OF i.
    DATA lv_out1  TYPE i.
    DATA lv_m     TYPE numc2.
    DATA lv_d     TYPE numc2.

    iv_jy = iv_shamsi_date(4).
    iv_jm = iv_shamsi_date+5(2).
    iv_jd = iv_shamsi_date+8(2).

    lv_jy = iv_jy + 1595.

    lv_out2 = -355668 + ( 365 * lv_jy ) +
              ( ( lv_jy DIV 33 ) * 8 ) +
              ( ( ( lv_jy MOD 33 ) + 3 ) DIV 4 ) +
              iv_jd +
              COND i( WHEN iv_jm < 7
                      THEN ( iv_jm - 1 ) * 31
                      ELSE ( ( iv_jm - 7 ) * 30 ) + 186 ).

    lv_out0 = 400 * ( lv_out2 DIV 146097 ).
    lv_out2 = lv_out2 MOD 146097.

    IF lv_out2 > 36524.
      lv_out2 -= 1.
      lv_out0 += ( 100 * ( lv_out2 DIV 36524 ) ).
      lv_out2 = lv_out2 MOD 36524.
      IF lv_out2 >= 365.
        lv_out2 += 1.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    lv_out0 += ( 4 * ( lv_out2 DIV 1461 ) ).
    lv_out2 = lv_out2 MOD 1461.

    IF lv_out2 > 365.
      lv_out0 += ( ( lv_out2 - 1 ) DIV 365 ).
      lv_out2 = ( lv_out2 - 1 ) MOD 365.
    ENDIF.

    " Calculate leap year
    lv_leap = COND #( WHEN ( lv_out0 MOD 4 = 0 AND lv_out0 MOD 100 <> 0 )
                        OR ( lv_out0 MOD 400 = 0 )
                      THEN 29
                      ELSE 28 ).

    " Initialize days per month array with leap year consideration
    lt_sal_a = VALUE #( ( 0 )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( lv_leap )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( 30 )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( 30 )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( 30 )
                        ( 31 )
                        ( 30 )
                        ( 31 ) ).

    lv_out2 += 1.
    lv_out1 = 0.
    DATA(lv_indx) = 1.
    WHILE lv_out1 < 13 AND lv_out2 > lt_sal_a[ lv_indx + 1 ].
      lv_out2 -= lt_sal_a[ lv_indx + 1 ].
      lv_out1 += 1.
      lv_indx += 1.
    ENDWHILE.

    lv_m = lv_out1 + 1.
    lv_d = lv_out2.

    ev_greg_date = |{ lv_out0 }{ lv_m }{ lv_d }|.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD first_dayof_month.
    DATA shamci_dt TYPE c LENGTH 10.
    DATA month     TYPE n LENGTH 2.
    DATA year      TYPE n LENGTH 4.

    TRY.
        convert_gregorian_to_shamsi( EXPORTING iv_date        = p_date
                                     IMPORTING ev_shamsi_date = shamci_dt ).

      CATCH cx_root.
        MESSAGE e002(zhr_py) WITH p_date
                RAISING date_conversion_error.
    ENDTRY.

    month = shamci_dt+5(2).
    year = shamci_dt+0(4).

    CONCATENATE year '/' month '/01' INTO shamci_dt.

    TRY.
        convert_shamsi_to_gregorian( EXPORTING iv_shamsi_date = shamci_dt
                                     IMPORTING ev_greg_date   = p_first_days ).

      CATCH cx_root.
        MESSAGE e002(zhr_py) WITH p_date
                RAISING date_conversion_error.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD first_dayof_year.
    DATA shamci_dt TYPE c LENGTH 10.
    DATA year      TYPE n LENGTH 4.

    TRY.

        convert_gregorian_to_shamsi( EXPORTING iv_date        = p_date
                                     IMPORTING ev_shamsi_date = shamci_dt ).

      CATCH cx_root.
        MESSAGE e002(zhr_py) WITH p_date
                RAISING date_conversion_error.
    ENDTRY.

    year = shamci_dt+0(4).
    CONCATENATE year '/01/01' INTO shamci_dt.

    TRY.
        convert_shamsi_to_gregorian( EXPORTING iv_shamsi_date = shamci_dt
                                     IMPORTING ev_greg_date   = p_first_days ).

      CATCH cx_root.
        MESSAGE e002(zhr_py) WITH p_date
                RAISING date_conversion_error.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD get_cal_days_inyrear.
    DATA first_days TYPE d.

    first_dayof_year( EXPORTING p_date       = p_date
                      IMPORTING p_first_days = first_days ).

    CALL FUNCTION 'HR_99S_INTERVAL_BETWEEN_DATES'
      EXPORTING
        begda = first_days
        endda = p_date
      IMPORTING
        days  = p_days.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD last_dayof_month.
    DATA shamci_dt TYPE c LENGTH 10.
    DATA month     TYPE n LENGTH 2.
    DATA year      TYPE n LENGTH 4.

    TRY.
        convert_gregorian_to_shamsi( EXPORTING iv_date        = p_date
                                     IMPORTING ev_shamsi_date = shamci_dt ).

      CATCH cx_root.
        MESSAGE e002(zhr_py) WITH p_date
                RAISING date_conversion_error.
    ENDTRY.

    month = shamci_dt+5(2).
    year = shamci_dt+0(4).

    IF month <> '12'.
      month += 1.

    ELSE.
      month = '01'.
      year += 1.
    ENDIF.

    CONCATENATE year '/' month '/01' INTO shamci_dt.

    TRY.
        convert_shamsi_to_gregorian( EXPORTING iv_shamsi_date = shamci_dt
                                     IMPORTING ev_greg_date   = p_last_days ).

      CATCH cx_root.
        MESSAGE e002(zhr_py) WITH p_date
                RAISING date_conversion_error.
    ENDTRY.
    CALL FUNCTION 'RP_CALC_DATE_IN_INTERVAL'
      EXPORTING
        date      = p_last_days
        days      = 1
        months    = 0
        signum    = '-'
        years     = 0
      IMPORTING
        calc_date = p_last_days.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Widgets in SAP Business One HANA retrieves informatio...
entry.content: Hello Matija, thank you for your response, I have executed the queries on 'database B,' and it indeed brings the correct information. The queries and the native calculated views are pointing to the correct database. However, the visual results of the KPIs and widgets in general are pulling information from 'database A.' Is there any way to reload the native widgets again?

entry.title: Re: 🇦🇪 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Dubai, UAE)
entry.content:
Dear @eshrinivasan / @ajmaradiaga ,

I would like to extend my heartfelt gratitude to both of you for the outstanding SAP CodeJam event held on 26th March in Dubai, focused on SAP Build Apps. Your presentation and delivery were nothing short of exceptional. It’s not just my personal observation—I've gathered feedback from participants as well, covering both the session and the arrangements, and the responses have been overwhelmingly positive and appreciative.

It was truly impressive to see your enthusiasm and energy remain consistently high throughout the full-day event, even into the evening. Your dedication and expertise left a lasting impression on the audience, and I am confident that this event has played a key role in expanding the SAP community in the Middle East.

Looking forward to your continued support and collaboration in upcoming events.

cc: @KatherineKenneally

entry.title: Re: EC Promotion - Promote link in worksheet is not avail...

Just for future reference, I did as you mentioned before.

Basically, all the fiels are Read-Only - No and the FBPs are configured to edit to all the fields available and related to Promotion.

entry.title: Re: SAC CSS Input Control Properties dont work
entry.content:
@DenisTthank you.
This didnt work:

.inputcontrol .sap-custom-input-control-mark-border {border-color: rgb(255,0,0);}

This worked:

.inputcontrol .sap-custom-input-control-widget {background-color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #000000}

entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: 採購單與供應商發票報表
entry.content: Question forwarded to experts. Hope it can be answered soon.

entry.title: Re: New Intelligent Look Up Feature in SAP Datasphere.
entry.content:
i never use them as there are these limitations:

- they need to be scheduled:

- they need to be a source for other view (not an intermediate step).

the only use case is to augument a master data with another master data.

For example customer master data with addresses with data from different systems, if we think about S/4HANA table schema, it is coherent so you do not need to use these fuzzy rules instead of well defined joins, while between different system you may need these fuzzy rules.

But with transactional data the limitations above means they are not usefull, for example, add a lookup on a transactional data with million of rows, just the fact that the output needs physicization and it can not be an intermediate step in a view but it need to be a source of the view => it means is not practical at all to use.

entry.title: Re: GR Print Preview in Public Cloud
entry.content: Dear Customer, Thanks for the details shared, this feature is not supported in current release now.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Visibility of Input Controls in SAP Analytics...
entry.content:
Hi,

yes that´s possible. Let´s assume you have slider called "Slider_1", which allows values between 0 and 100 and whenever the slider is equal or above 50 you want to hide the input control called "InputControl_1" (and it should be visible otherwise, of course). You now need an "onChange" event coding on the slider (just click on the three dots next to the slider and select "Edit scripts".
Here´s an easy coding for the onChange script:

if (Slider_1.getValue() >= 50) {
	InputControl_1.setVisible(false);
} else {
	InputControl_1.setVisible(true);
}

Please notice, that you can improve this, by not hiding an already invisible input control, but I wanted to keep it simple here.

If the solution answers your question, please set it to "accepted solution". Thanks.

Kind regards,
Denis

entry.title: Re: ABAP OOP Syntax help
entry.content: @Sandra, thanks for your time, I can do oop, I just haven't explored the available methods, when I saw the GET_SELECTED_ROWS_ID_BASE Raghavendran already posted it here. Appreciate the time.

entry.title: The partial write-off of the fixed asset with quantity, error FAA_Post081
entry.content: The original cost of the fixed asset in the evaluation area 01 is 0, and there are revaluation amounts. When partially transferring or writing off the fixed asset with quantity, the error FAA_Post081 occurs. If the transaction is carried out without specifying the quantity (using percentages or amounts), it is processed successfully. However, the requirement is for the quantity to be included in the journal entry. Could you please advise how to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: How to Create MRP Area and mapping with Product material master App
entry.content: Move the case to tag with material master.

entry.title: Re: SAC CSS Input Control Properties dont work
entry.content:
Hi @Alex_B,
perfect. I assumed by your first post that you wanted to change the border color of the checkbox, not of the whole input control. But great that you managed that!
Kind regards,
Denis

entry.title: BAPI_BOM_UPLOAD_SAVE VALID_DATE ERROR
entry.content:
Dear Expert

I use BAPI - BAPI_BOM_UPLOAD_SAVE to create a BOM. From Postman transfer data to SAP BAPI.

But Valid_FROM always is Today. I can not use other Date.

Is there any expert could tell me the root cause?

thanks for your help !!

Best Regards,

Connie

entry.title: Re: ABAP OOP Syntax help
entry.content: @Raghavendran_Mohandoss spot on ! this is what I need, I was checking how to access the data from the path I provided when I checked other methods from this class and found it. I haven't tested it but when I saw your answer to this thread then you have confirmed my thoughts. Thank you sir.

entry.title: Notification during statuses change
entry.content: Do candidates receive notification when their statuses change in the application? For example, when the status changes from "New application" to "Shortlist" and similar?
